mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 3 months signals rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 2 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to increased wage pressures and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 1.5-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over 3 months signals an increase in supply-side pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and a 1% decrease in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over 2 months signal a monetary tightening cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL falls < 1% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1.5-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary policy periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the RSAFS (Retail Sales) over 2 months and a 0.5% decrease in the UNRATE (Unemployment Rate) over 2 months signal a strengthening consumer sector, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 1% and UNRATE falls < 0.5% in 2 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1.5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the PAYEMS (Nonfarm Payroll Employment) over 2 months and a 0.5% increase in the HOUST (Housing Starts) over 2 months signal a strengthening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 1% and HOUST rises > 0.5% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decrease in the GDPC1 (Gross Domestic Product) over 3 months signals a decrease in supply-side pressures, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.
